threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 10 minutes late is too late; everyone here shows up whenever. Time's a stretch, not a deadline.
Caught a lad singing to his reflection in the bus window near Brixton Academy. Didn’t know whether to clap or offer him a tissue.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a whole family of wild rabbits casually grazing in my garden while the neighbor played accordion, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn trains sometimes stop for a minute while the driver just chats with the station staff? Friends catching up mid-commute.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their casual disdain for the Muni delays—most non-locals blame the bus, while we just shrug and check our phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the excitement at Civic Center when a dog on the tracks turned the whole station into a live-action comedy. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all the plazas are for mingling, but locals see them as quiet escape spots between errands, not social hubs.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a new arrival by the way they wait for the tram, checking the app repeatedly instead of enjoying a coffee at the stop.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check your phone for the bus times instead of just walking over to the stop and checking the board. Classic newbie move.
Caught the 196 home last night, and there was a guy in a bright pink onesie singing to himself. Only in Brixton, right? Classic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a crowd of folks having a spontaneous dance-off on Weserstraße while the BVG buses were on strike. Pure Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a currywurst stand that doubles as an art gallery. Grab a bite while admiring local street art on rotating panels.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know which taco truck has the best birria at 2 AM, instead of just pointing to the fancy spots.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 6 tonight with a saxophonist serenading the packed bus. Only in San Francisco do you get a live concert mid-commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just about the parks and plazas, but it's the quiet little street art tucked away in alleys that really tells the neighborhood's story.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their confused look when they see a tapenade jar in the market. Locals know it's not the same as pisto.
